text:
The bag quality is really good and feels sturdy. The outer shell is strong, the design looks premium, and the wheels move smoothly overall. Size and storage are perfect for travel, and the finish is neat and classy.

The only reason for giving 4 stars instead of 5 is that there are minor scratches on the wheels, likely due to handling or drop point during delivery. It doesn’t affect functionality at all, but worth mentioning.

Overall, a great product at this price, durable and worth buying. Highly recommended for frequent travellers.

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
text:
The build quality is really super and feels sturdy. Wheels are smooth and easy to move, even when fully loaded. The outer shell looks premium and durable.
However, the major drawback is the lack of internal layers or compartments. There is no proper separation or spacing to organize items—it’s basically one large open area like a box. Because of this, keeping clothes and accessories neatly arranged becomes difficult.
Overall, it’s good for carrying large quantities, but not ideal if you prefer organized packing.

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
text:
I recently bought the Safari Thorium Neo 8 Wheels 66 cm medium check-in suitcase and it has exceeded my expectations on every trip! The hard polycarbonate shell feels tough and has protected my luggage well even after multiple journeys — it doesn’t show scratches easily and still looks great. �
YouTube +1
The 8-wheel 360° spinner system is a big highlight — it glides effortlessly through airports, tight aisles, and hotel lobbies without catching or wobbling. Makes traveling so smooth! �
Amazon India
Inside, the bag is very spacious, with enough room for everything I needed on a week-long trip. The fixed combination lock gives good peace of mind, and the textured finish adds a premium look. �
India Free Stuff
Overall, this suitcase feels well-built for the price and is perfect for frequent flyers, family vacations, or business travel. Excellent value for money — definitely recommended! ✈️

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
text:
I recently purchased this Mokobara polycarbonate cabin suitcase and the experience has been excellent.

The build quality is solid and premium. The polycarbonate shell feels sturdy yet lightweight. The zipper quality and inner fabric finishing are top-notch and clearly well thought through. The wheels glide smoothly, and the overall design is both functional and elegant.

Everything about the product reflects attention to detail and good manufacturing standards. It is definitely value for money and a reliable travel companion.

Highly recommended.

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
text:
Mokobara delivered as expected, the bag has a premium finish with very good built quality, the chains were of highest standard and the wheels are very smooth. The telescopic handle has 3 levels and also of good quality. The best is the easily accessible extra compartment for laptop and other important documents.
The price is a little bit on the higher side but the quality of the product will live up to it. This review is my first impression of the product, will review it more after using it on a trip.
One little setback is or i would say icing on the cake would be to provide some free travel organising bags and an usb charging port which other brand offer at this price range.
Overall, if your budget is 7k go for it without second thoughts.
Update : After using it for a year, I’ve found the trolley to be sturdy and well-balanced. However, one drawback is that it easily exceeds the 7 kg limit, with very less content. especially when packing electronics like a laptop and power banks that are already heavy on their own. Additionally, the front compartment tends to push inward, reducing the main storage space inside.

title: 3.0 out of 5 stars
text: The overall build quality of the bag are actually quite good. However, the moving parts are a bit disappointing. The wheels are noisier than expected. Additionally, the telescopic handle feels flimsy and doesn't match the quality I expected at this price point. It’s a decent bag, but the hardware needs an upgrade.
